source_quote: Table 1: Drinking Days at 3-month follow-up: ALC 27.5 (12.9), Control 29.2 (18.9). N per condition estimated from total N=154 across 4 conditions (see Figure 1). Four-arm study (Control, ALC, HIV, ALC+HIV); Alcohol risk content main effect on drinking intensity F(1,131)=4.58, p=.034, eta2=.028. Alcohol condition vs Control on drinks per drinking day F(1,131)=6.82, p=.010, eta2=.050.

Table 3: At 12 months, 45% of the intervention group were marijuana abstinent as measured by TLFB, compared to 22% of the assessed controls (OR 2.89, 95%CI 1.22, 6.84, p<0.014). I: 21/47 abstinent, AC: 12/55 abstinent.
Table 4: Intervention group: 47 followed, 21 lost to follow-up out of 68 randomized. Assessed Control group: 55 followed, 16 lost to follow-up out of 71 randomized at 12 months.

Table 2: Mean (SD) Number of Days of Marijuana Use in the Last 60 Days at 3-month follow-up: MET 31.05 (23.28), DFC 32.76 (20.61). N=93 total (MET n=47, DFC n=50). Between-groups effect size d=0.08, not significant. The hypothesized Condition x Time interaction was not significant.
